behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert very specific literal strings like 'inner class Value(val value: T)', 'state.continuation.resume(Value(value))', 'handleActions<Value>', '__continuation(false, null)', '__continuation(true, _result.value)', 'convertBlockPtrToKotlinFunction<(kotlin.Boolean, kotlin.native.internal.NativePtr)->Unit>', '{ arg0, arg1 in', 'if arg0 {', 'continuation(.some(element))', 'continuation(.none)', and 'int32_t (^continuation)(bool, void * _Nullable )'. While the instruction describes the general

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly states the fix: 'Add an inner Value wrapper class', 'Change emit() to wrap values in Value(value) before resuming', 'Change next() return type from T? to Value?', 'Update the C bridge function to pass a boolean flag plus value', 'Change the continuation callback signature from (void * _Nullable) to (bool, void * _Nullable)', etc. This describes exactly WHAT to change, not just what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact class name 'inner class Value(val value: T)', exact method call 'state.continuation.resume(Value(value))', exact variable names 'handleActions<Value>', '_result.value', 'arg0, arg1', exact strings like '__continuation(false, null)' and '__continuation(true, _result.value)'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. a sealed class, Optional wrapper, Result type, or different variable names would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every fail-to-pass test is purely text matching: 'assert "inner class Value(val value: T)" in content', 'assert "state.continuation.resume(Value(value))" in content', etc. Only test_swift_format_syntax runs a subprocess (swift-format lint). No test actually compiles or executes the code to verify the nullable flow behavior works correctly.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since all fail-to-pass tests are string-matching (e.g., checking 'inner class Value(val value: T)' exists in a file), an agent could simply insert the expected strings as comments or dead code without implementing any actual fix, and all tests would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md states the expected behavior: 'a Flow<Elem?> that emits [Element1, null, Element2, null, Element3] should correctly produce all 5 elements when consumed from Swift via asAsyncSequence()'. No test verifies this runtime behavior — tests only check for specific strings in source files.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (e.g., test_value_wrapper_class_exists, test_next_function_returns_value_type) with clear docstrings indicating FAIL-TO-PASS vs PASS-TO-PASS, and each test has a descriptive assertion message.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No apparent typos in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact implementation details: 'Add an inner Value wrapper class to wrap emitted values', 'Change emit() to wrap values in Value(value) before resuming', 'Change next() return type from T? to Value?', specific callback signature changes. This goes well beyond identifying the buggy area.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-editing task, not a config-editing task.
